steps.title: Apply margin of error to find the confidence interval
steps.content: Margin of error defines the range around a sample statistic where we estimate the population parameter lies. With a sample mean of 37 and margin of error of 3, the confidence interval is: $$37 \pm 3$$

steps.title: Calculate the interval bounds
steps.content:
Computing the bounds:
Lower: $37 - 3 = 34$
Upper: $37 + 3 = 40$

The population mean is estimated to be between 34 and 40. That matches choice C — C is correct.
